Angular v22 introduced a networking change that many developers may never notice — and that’s exactly the point.
If you’ve been configuring your application with withFetch(), you can now remove it entirely. Starting with Angular v22, HttpClient automatically uses the Fetch API, making the old opt-in configuration unnecessary.
The best part?
For the majority of applications, nothing else changes. Your existing HTTP requests continue to work exactly as before while Angular switches to a more modern implementation behind the scenes.
Let’s look at what changed, when you need to care, and the one scenario where you’ll still want to keep XMLHttpRequest.
What Changed?
Before Angular v22, developers who wanted to use the Fetch API explicitly enabled it.
bootstrapApplication(MainComponent, {
providers: [
provideHttpClient(
withFetch()
)
]
});With Angular v22, Fetch is already enabled.
bootstrapApplication(MainComponent, {
providers: [
provideHttpClient()
]
});That’s it.

• No migration script.
